paragraphs.text: Hi,
I am an EU citizen and I have a few suggestions to make digital fairness in the EU even better. In short, they are:

evidence_quotes.quote: Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

paragraphs.text: Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: pressuring businesses on what goods and services they are allowed to provide and sell.

paragraphs.text: Require payment processors to facilitate commerce of legal goods and services indiscriminately and prevent them from influencing and/or pressuring businesses on what goods and services they are allowed to provide and sell.

evidence_quotes.quote: return any and all funds stored on a user's account in case the account is closed for any reason.

paragraphs.text: Require payment processors to return any and all funds stored on a user's account in case the account is closed for any reason.

evidence_quotes.quote: Require Internet browsers to be able to automatically answer cookie prompts on websites based on user settings.

paragraphs.text: Require Internet browsers to be able to automatically answer cookie prompts on websites based on user settings.
Require cookie prompts on websites to display standardized buttons and prompt layout to users..
Protect encryption by forbidding mandatory vulnerabilities, back doors and "secure legal access" features.

evidence_quotes.quote: prohibiting scanning of correspondence, messages and communication before encrypting and after decrypting by default.

paragraphs.text: Protect and maintain secrecy of correspondence and privacy of EU citizens by prohibiting scanning of correspondence, messages and communication before encrypting and after decrypting by default. Allow only limited, targeted and temporary exceptions for crime fighting.

evidence_quotes.quote: protect citizens' privacy and anonymity by providing minimal amount of data to websites and/or third-parties.

paragraphs.text: Require age verification schemes to protect citizens' privacy and anonymity by providing minimal amount of data to websites and/or third-parties.

evidence_quotes.quote: platform independent
available also from community sources
it can be installed by everyone

paragraphs.text: Ensure the EU's age verification app is platform independent, is available also from community sources and that it can be installed by everyone.
Ensure the EU's age verification app is fully usable and installable on versions of Android that are not licensed or approved by Google.

evidence_quotes.quote: give a clear reason and a reference to a specific rule of the platform that was broken by the user

paragraphs.text: Require online platforms to give a clear reason and a reference to a specific rule of the platform that was broken by the user when user's account is banned, suspended, closed or otherwise negatively affected by the platform.

evidence_quotes.quote: Require platforms to provide a genuine opportunity to their users to appeal the platform's decision to close, ban or suspend user's account.

paragraphs.text: Require platforms to provide a genuine opportunity to their users to appeal the platform's decision to close, ban or suspend user's account.

evidence_quotes.quote: use the Open Document Format (ODF)

paragraphs.text: Require EU organizations and EU member states' governments, local governments and any public and private non-governmental organizations (NGOs) to support, promote and use the Open Document Format (ODF) in order to achieve digital sovereignty.

evidence_quotes.quote: released to the public under a Free Software license

paragraphs.text: Require all software that is developed with public funds to be released to the public under a Free Software license which ensures the public can freely use, study, share and modify the software their taxes pay for.

evidence_quotes.quote: hindering customers and third-parties from installing custom operating systems, applications, firmware and device drivers

paragraphs.text: Prevent hardware manufacturers from hindering customers and third-parties from installing custom operating systems, applications, firmware and device drivers on products, such as desktop computers, laptop computers, single board computers, phones, tablets and embedded devices like internet routers and wireless access points.

evidence_quotes.quote: Require hardware manufacturers to provide detailed, free and open documentation for consumers and third-parties on how alternative operating systems, applications, firmware and device drivers can be installed on their products, such as desktop computers, laptop computers,

paragraphs.text: Require hardware manufacturers to provide detailed, free and open documentation for consumers and third-parties on how alternative operating systems, applications, firmware and device drivers can be installed on their products, such as desktop computers, laptop computers,
Feedback for Digital Fairness Act 1 / 18

evidence_quotes.quote: Protect citizen's rights to refuse ads and block tracking
Require advertisements to accurately represent products, make reporting misleading advertisements easier on digital platforms and increase consequences of false advertising
Guarantee legality of ad blockers

paragraphs.text: single board computers, phones, tablets and embedded devices like internet routers and wireless access points...11 17. Require hardware manufacturers to provide detailed, free and open technical documentation on how internal hardware components they use work, such as Wi-Fi chips on computer motherboards, so that alternative software drivers can be developed for them...12 18. Require Internet Service Provides (ISPs) to allow consumers to choose to use their own routers instead of those offered by ISPs...13 19. Require device manufacturers to comply with the USB-C standard to ensure compatibility with third-party devices and prevent manufacturers from using non-compliant implementations in their products sold in the EU...13 20. Require advertisements to accurately represent products, make reporting misleading advertisements easier on digital platforms and increase consequences of false advertising...14 21. Protect citizen's rights to refuse ads and block tracking...14 22. Guarantee legality of ad blockers...15 23. Prevent platforms and service providers from discriminating against businesses who involve themselves with only legal content...15 24. Use only European companies, solutions and services for EU institutions' data storage, software solutions and cloud infrastructure...16 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers...16 26. Require black-and-white printing to only use black ink instead of colour cartridges...17 27. Ensure consumers can use all the ink and/or toner stored within their printer cartridges...17 28. Forbid tipping in the EU...17
Please, find more details about my suggestions below:
Feedback for Digital Fairness Act 2 / 18

evidence_quotes.quote: This is because they require a connection to game publisher's servers which no longer exist.
Also, brand new games that I buy today will similarly become unplayable when the servers go down permanently in the near future.

paragraphs.text: I play video games as a hobby. I have done so from childhood and I enjoy them very much. My old video games from the nineties still work. However, many newer games I come across do not. This is because they require a connection to game publisher's servers which no longer exist. Also, brand new games that I buy today will similarly become unplayable when the servers go down permanently in the near future.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: This is how many video games are designed to function these days.
They require access to resources outside of the game itself which, eventually, will disappear.
After that, the game won't work anymore despite not being broken.
This is planned obsolescence and violates my right to own property.

paragraphs.text: In my opinion, this is like a book publisher printing books with slowly fading ink. Eventually, the books will be unreadable. This is how many video games are designed to function these days. They require access to resources outside of the game itself which, eventually, will disappear. After that, the game won't work anymore despite not being broken. What a great harm to culture and consumer rights (specifically Article 17 §1 of the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union). This is planned obsolescence and violates my right to own property.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: video games
They can choose to design their games with an End of Life plan in mind.

This plan is unique to each game, but in essence, it ensures that the game can be played without requiring support, connection, approval or anything from the original developers or publishers after they decide to stop supporting the game.

paragraphs.text: Fortunately, video games don't have to be made this way. It is a choice made by the developers and/or publishers. They can choose to design their games with an End of Life plan in mind. This plan is unique to each game, but in essence, it ensures that the game can be played without requiring support, connection, approval or anything from the original developers or publishers after they decide to stop supporting the game.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: It would be very hard to modify existing games like this, but it is easy to design new games with this in mind.
An End Of Life plan would, of course, free developers and publishers from any responsibility to offer any kind of support after the game is left in a playable state and the support ends.
It would be unfair, after all, to require endless support.

paragraphs.text: It would be very hard to modify existing games like this, but it is easy to design new games with this in mind. An End Of Life plan would, of course, free developers and publishers from any responsibility to offer any kind of support after the game is left in a playable state and the support ends. It would be unfair, after all, to require endless support.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: video games sold in the EU
End of Life Plan
planned obsolescence

paragraphs.text: It would be very fair to require all video games sold in the EU to be developed with an End of Life Plan. Today, they are designed with planned obsolescence in their core. I think this is very unfair to all consumers and violates my rights to own the games I buy. For even more details, please see European Citizen's Initiative titled "Stop Destroying Videogames" (ECI(2024)000007).

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: keep using any digital product they have bought

paragraphs.text: It would be very fair to consumers if they could keep using any digital product they have bought like movies, books and music after the products are no longer available to purchase or the publishers and/or digital platforms the products are sold on are no longer in business.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: video games

paragraphs.text: If we lose art, such as video games, to planned obsolescence, we will lose a big part of our selves. Please, help.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

paragraphs.text: Feedback for Digital Fairness Act 3 / 18

paragraphs.text: Require payment processors to facilitate commerce of legal goods and services indiscriminately and prevent them from influencing and/or pressuring businesses on what goods and services they are allowed to provide and sell.

evidence_quotes.quote: Visa had pressured them to remove certain games from their website.
If itch.io had not complied, Visa would have limited or terminated their services to this platform.

paragraphs.text: On 28th July 2025, a digital platform and market place for video games called "itch.io" announced that their payment provider (Visa) had pressured them to remove certain games from their website. If itch.io had not complied, Visa would have limited or terminated their services to this platform. This would have significantly impacted their business, as Visa is one of the biggest payment processors available. Itch.oi had no choice but to comply with Visa's demands.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: must not be able to affect what legal products and services businesses can provide and what legal products and services customers can purchase.

paragraphs.text: The games in question do contain adult only material, but are legal to make and sell. In my opinion, EU should require that all payment processors, banks and any other actors between a business and a consumer, such as delivery companies, must not be able to affect what legal products and services businesses can provide and what legal products and services customers can purchase. Only local and EU laws determine what is illegal or otherwise not allowed.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: They can heavily restrict and censor what consumers can buy if they abuse their position like Visa did with itch.io.
MasterCard and PayPal have done similar censorship and with a video game platform called "Steam" lately.

paragraphs.text: American payment processors like Visa, MasterCard and PayPal have a very dominant market share. They can heavily restrict and censor what consumers can buy if they abuse their position like Visa did with itch.io. MasterCard and PayPal have done similar censorship and with a video game platform called "Steam" lately. Practices like these are expected to get more frequent. This is very unfair and both EU businesses and consumers need EU's protection against this. There needs to be a clear law stating that this is an illegal business practice.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: Is it justifiable for payment processors to be able to deny "brand damaging" transactions that are fully legal?

paragraphs.text: It seems that Visa was convinced to pressure itch.io due to being contacted by a pro-censorship activist group. Some of the games they managed to remove contained LGBTQ+ themes. Is it justifiable for payment processors to be able to deny "brand damaging" transactions that are fully legal? I don't think so. As a matter of fact, I think it is unfair to say the least.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: censoring and limiting consumer choices on a whim of a single corporate executive?

paragraphs.text: What prevents payment processors from censoring and limiting consumer choices on a whim of a single corporate executive? What prevents payment processors from being used as a leverage by American government against the EU? What prevents payment processors from limiting citizen's options to supports NGOs or charities that payment processors, American government and/or other third-parties don't like? Not much. Or so they think at least.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

paragraphs.text: Please, protect our rights to buy legal goods and services. Please, protect our rights to donate to causes we see fit. Please, protect EU businesses from unfair and unethical business practices, pressure and censorship from payment processors. Please make sure that any other financial actors like banks cannot do the same as payment processors are doing right now as described above.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

paragraphs.text: In summary, please, protect our rights to transaction neutrality.

evidence_quotes.quote: If true, this is beyond unfair. This is theft.
Please, require that payment processors immediately return all funds to the user when their account is closed for any reason.

paragraphs.text: I have heard of an incident where a PayPal user's account was closed by PayPal and the user's funds stored on that account were not returned to the user. If true, this is beyond unfair. This is theft. Please, require that payment processors immediately return all funds to the user when their account is closed for any reason.

paragraphs.text: Please, don't let payment processors to steal our money.

evidence_quotes.quote: automatically answer cookie prompts on websites based on user settings.

paragraphs.text: Require Internet browsers to be able to automatically answer cookie prompts on websites based on user settings.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: Some times the buttons to accept only necessary cookies or to reject all cookies are hidden in a "settings" section or are simply missing completely.

paragraphs.text: I am frequently annoyed by cookie prompts when I visit websites. I have to read the prompt very carefully and manually select what cookies I consent to. Some times the buttons to accept only necessary cookies or to reject all cookies are hidden in a "settings" section or are simply missing completely. Treating consumers like this is unfair, especially when giving or denying consent could be automated in the browser settings.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: automatic answers to cookie prompts in the browser settings.
These settings would correspond to clicking a button on a cookie prompt like "accept all cookies", "accept only necessary cookies" or "reject all cookies".

paragraphs.text: EU should require browsers like Chrome, Edge and Firefox to allow users to optionally define automatic answers to cookie prompts in the browser settings. These settings would correspond to clicking a button on a cookie prompt like "accept all cookies", "accept only necessary cookies" or "reject all cookies". The browser would then deal with all cookie prompts automatically as the user has configured.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

evidence_quotes.quote: to not get a cookie prompt when visiting a website while also having my consent respected.
open and freely available communication standard between the browser and the cookie prompts so that all browsers and cookie prompts can be made compatible with this automation.
EU regulation could be an option to achieve uniformity of technical design, high compatibility and high adoption on websites.

paragraphs.text: This would remove a lot of unnecessary hassle from the point of view of a consumer. It would be so nice to not get a cookie prompt when visiting a website while also having my consent respected. Of course, this requires an open and freely available communication standard between the browser and the cookie prompts so that all browsers and cookie prompts can be made compatible with this automation. This can be very difficult to achieve technically if left to the industry resulting in various incompatible solutions. In this regard, EU regulation could be an option to achieve uniformity of technical design, high compatibility and high adoption on websites.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

paragraphs.text: Please, make the prompts less annoying.

evidence_quotes.quote: buttons on the cookie prompts should be standardized.
The buttons "accept all cookies", "accept only necessary cookies" and "reject all cookies" should all have the same labels, be always visible and require only one click to activate

paragraphs.text: Additionally, or if the above automation is not feasible, the buttons on the cookie prompts should be standardized. The buttons "accept all cookies", "accept only necessary cookies" and "reject all cookies" should all have the same labels, be always visible and require only one click to activate

paragraphs.text: If encryption, like communication security between banks and consumers, is weakened by an official and mandatory vulnerability or "back door" for any reason, then the encryption in question is fundamentally not fit for purpose. It becomes completely insecure and unusable, because any back door, no matter how well secured and hidden, will be found and exploited by criminals and hostile foreign agents. Granting the authorities an ability to break our encryption we rely on, takes away our security and privacy.

6. Protect encryption by forbidding mandatory vulnerabilities, back doors and "secure legal access" features.

evidence_quotes.quote: Mandating broken encryption

paragraphs.text: As a citizen, I will lose trust in a bank, a government agency or an online retailer who uses insecure methods to encrypt my data and communication. Mandating broken encryption is very unfair and dangerous to businesses, citizens and society as a whole.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
6. Protect encryption by forbidding mandatory vulnerabilities, back doors and "secure legal access" features.

paragraphs.text: There is no secure way to break encryption. Please, don't take our security away. That is not fair at all. Protect it instead, please. Forbid mandatory vulnerabilities, back doors and "secure legal access" features in encryption and all software that use encryption.

evidence_quotes.quote: the right of secrecy of correspondence.
Same should apply online as well.

paragraphs.text: In real life, in other words offline, citizens enjoy the right of secrecy of correspondence. By default, our letters and messages are not read by government officials or other third-parties, but only by the intended recipients. Same should apply online as well.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

7. Protect and maintain secrecy of correspondence and privacy of EU citizens by prohibiting scanning of correspondence, messages and communication before encrypting and after decrypting by default. Allow only limited, targeted and temporary exceptions for crime fighting.

paragraphs.text: Officials may deem it necessary to read certain messages to fight crime. This can be done only as an exception and must always require a court order granting a temporary and limited permit to access specific citizen's correspondence. Same should apply online. Only when law enforcement suspects that a crime is taking place or is being planned, can they break the secrecy of correspondence. Not whenever they like, not by default and not for all citizens at once.

paragraphs.text: Please, protect our right to secrecy of correspondence online by implementing restrictions and conditions for braking it equivalent to offline restrictions. Please, do not allow mass surveillance and loss of secrecy of correspondence and loss of privacy by default. Please, allow only limited and temporary exceptions backed by court orders.

If we want them to be effective, we have to force users to use their official government ID or something similar as verification. I worry that too much information is provided to the website or a third-party the website works with to implement the verification.

evidence_quotes.quote: I don't feel comfortable giving my name, social security number, address or a picture of my face to any website or various third-parties who I cannot verify are reliable and secure actors with my data.
The possibility of my data being leaked or illegally accessed is terrifying.
Criminals will steal sensitive data.
They will plant malware to spy on users.

paragraphs.text: I don't feel comfortable giving my name, social security number, address or a picture of my face to any website or various third-parties who I cannot verify are reliable and secure actors with my data. I feel especially uncomfortable if this site is an adults only site. The possibility of my data being leaked or illegally accessed is terrifying. It would put me in great risk of becoming a victim of a crime such as extortion or identity theft. I worry that sites and/or third-parties handling and/or storing sensitive data will eventually get broken into as well. Criminals will steal sensitive data. They will plant malware to spy on users.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
8. Require age verification schemes to protect citizens' privacy and anonymity by providing minimal amount of data to websites and/or third-parties.

evidence_quotes.quote: no data that can leak from a website or from a third-party that can be used to profile and/or target me.
Minimization of provided data would also reduce the effectiveness of spyware on the website.

paragraphs.text: Therefore, I suggest that a website should only get a "yes or no" answer to the question "Is this user 18 or older?" from either an EU or national database containing citizens' personal information. Not my name or anything else. All I need to do is to log in to an EU or national age verification service when prompted by a website, accept that the site is provided the "yes" answer and continue browsing the site. The site should also not store this data. This way there is no data that can leak from a website or from a third-party that can be used to profile and/or target me. Minimization of provided data would also reduce the effectiveness of spyware on the website.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
8. Require age verification schemes to protect citizens' privacy and anonymity by providing minimal amount of data to websites and/or third-parties.

evidence_quotes.quote: Please, protect our privacy and anonymity.
this scheme requires citizens to provide a full national ID or a face scan to various third-parties and individual websites to verify their age.
no minimization of data either.

paragraphs.text: Please, protect our privacy and anonymity. I have heard that the UK failed to do this for their citizens when they introduced a new age verification scheme recently. Apparently, this scheme requires citizens to provide a full national ID or a face scan to various third-parties and individual websites to verify their age. Apparently, there is no secure and centralized data provider hosted by the UK and there is no minimization of data either. Absolutely terrifying and monstrously unfair. This has lead a lot of people to attempt to circumvent the verification scheme. Laws should not be so bad that people feel the need to break them to guarantee their safety and privacy.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
8. Require age verification schemes to protect citizens' privacy and anonymity by providing minimal amount of data to websites and/or third-parties.

paragraphs.text: In my opinion, anything this important should be vendor independent, widely available from multiple independent sources and not dependent on any American or non-EU companies. Therefore, I suggest, in the name of fairness, that EU's age verification app be available primarily on a centralized EU software repository as well as community platforms such as fdroid and only complimentarily on Google's Play Store and Apple's App Store. Everyone must be able to get this app if they so choose to.

paragraphs.text: These versions of Android are not licensed or approved by Google. They are built by a community of users using a freely available version of Android as a base. These Google-free versions of Android include at least "/e/OS", "Replicant", "LineageOS", "GrapheneOS" and "CalyxOS". evidence_quotes.quote: closed a user's account without giving a clear and specific reason why that happened.
a vague justification is given such as "Violation of Terms Of Service" without specifying what the violation was and what the specific terms were.

paragraphs.text: I have heard of many incidents where an online platform, such as YouTube, Twitch, X and Facebook, has closed a user's account without giving a clear and specific reason why that happened. Instead a vague justification is given such as "Violation of Terms Of Service" without specifying what the violation was and what the specific terms were. In these circumstances, trying to appeal the platform's decision is not adequately possible.

evidence_quotes.quote: Often, account bans and other punitive measures are automated and carried out by software bots.
Similarly, appeals are processed by bots which often disregard appeals which human reviewers would not.
Bots are used as there are not enough human employees available.

paragraphs.text: Appealing a decision on an online platform is often slow and unreliable. Often, account bans and other punitive measures are automated and carried out by software bots. Similarly, appeals are processed by bots which often disregard appeals which human reviewers would not. Bots are used as there are not enough human employees available. This leads to situations where users get punished more often than they should and appealing takes needlessly long as is too difficult.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
12. Require platforms to provide a genuine opportunity to their users to appeal the platform’s decision to close, ban or suspend user’s account.

evidence_quotes.quote: banned for unclear reasons or seemingly unjustifiably

paragraphs.text: For example, on YouTube, often an outcry from the user community is required for the platform to take corrective actions when a user’s account has been banned for unclear reasons or seemingly unjustifiably. Not all users get the community to notice their situation, and they are thus treated unequally.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.

paragraphs.text: I like Open Document Format (ODF) very much and prefer to use it over file formats like Microsoft’s OOXML. For example, I chose to write this very document as an Open Document Text file (.odt). This is because I can use ODF files with a lot of different software applications. Also, ODF is an open standard, which anyone can read and freely implement in their software without fees, licenses or needing to ask for permission.

evidence_quotes.quote: I can just use any modern software application that supports ODF and my files will just work.
Microsoft’s OOXML, on the other hand, is a secret not available to the public and fully controlled by Microsoft.
I fear that if I or anyone else stores their data relying on Microsoft’s secret and unreliable file formats, the data will be lost eventually.

paragraphs.text: This guarantees that my files and data will be accessible to me no matter how old my files are. Even if the software which I used to edit my files is no longer available or supported. I can just use any modern software application that supports ODF and my files will just work. Microsoft’s OOXML, on the other hand, is a secret not available to the public and fully controlled by Microsoft. I fear that if I or anyone else stores their data relying on Microsoft’s secret and unreliable file formats, the data will be lost eventually. This happens with all other secret file formats as well.

paragraphs.text: Promoting the use of ODF and other Open Standards would increase Europe's digital sovereignty by decreasing our reliance on foreign big tech companies and their file formats that they use to lock as in to their software ecosystems. paragraphs.text: I have many digital devices such as computers and smart phones. I often install custom operating systems, such as GNU/Linux on them, so that I can extend the devices' life span after support and security updates are no longer provided by the manufacturer. I also like to replace sub-optimal software with better one, so that my devices work better and provide the functionality that I want. Often, official software by the manufacturer lacks some functionality or features I prefer, or the software is too low quality to my liking. Fortunately, better alternatives exist.

evidence_quotes.quote: Unfortunately, manufacturers tend to artificially make their products very difficult to install custom operating systems or other software on them.

paragraphs.text: Unfortunately, manufacturers tend to artificially make their products very difficult to install custom operating systems or other software on them. Often they don't provide access to a bootloader that would let me start an operating system installer of my choice. Often, they prevent a device from turning on if the device detects an unofficial operating system has been installed. Such anti-consumer measures are harmful to me and other consumers. They also hinder third-party companies and competitors from offering their services, such as device repairs, operating system reinstallations and custom operating system installations.

paragraphs.text: I think these measures are unfair. They may enhance security, but they also hinder competition and decrease the level of ownership consumers have over their devices. Consumers and third-parties must be able to install custom operating system and other software on their devices if they so choose. Extending the usability and longevity of digital devices is good for consumers and the environment. A balance between security, manufacturer's business interests and consumer choice and ownership rights can be found. New security features or settings can be implemented that are more consumer-friendly.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ISP cannot mandate that we use only devices that they approve and/or offer.

paragraphs.text: In Finland, we can choose to keep our existing internet routers when we get a subscription for the internet from an ISP. We don't have to accept a new router the ISP may choose to offer to us. In case our device is too old or otherwise incompatible, we can purchase any router we like from any shop or provider we prefer. The ISP cannot mandate that we use only devices that they approve and/or offer. We have the freedom to choose.

paragraphs.text: I bought the Nintendo Switch gaming console in 2017. I liked it a lot and still play it to this day. However, soon after purchasing it, I run into an issue. I would have preferred to buy an extra docking station for it in case I travel. It is used to both charge the device's battery and to connect the device to a TV. Nintendo's offers were too expensive, in my opinion, and equivalent products from third-parties were cheaper. I thought it would be safe to choose one of the competing products.

evidence_quotes.quote: Nintendo did not fully comply with the USB-C standard

paragraphs.text: Unfortunately, it soon became known among the gaming community that Nintendo did not fully comply with the USB-C standard with their Nintendo Switch gaming console. The USB-C port on the gaming console and the USB-C plug on the docking station implement charging and/or power delivery differently than what is specified in the USB-C standard. Same goes for Nintendo's official charger that can be plugged in the gaming console when a docking station is not available.

Even if not done deliberately, this lowered trust on third-parties and their offerings even though they did nothing wrong. I myself ended up buying another docking station from Nintendo even though I would have preferred one from a third-party, because I feared that my gaming console would break if I used anything other than official Nintendo hardware with it.

evidence_quotes.quote: any manufacturer that includes a USB-C port on their product must make sure that it complies with the USB-C standard.
non-compliant implementations of the USB-C standard should be prevented by regulation.

paragraphs.text: So that similar cases can be avoided in the future, I think that any manufacturer that includes a USB-C port on their product must make sure that it complies with the USB-C standard. It is the only way to make sure that all standard compliant devices, whether first-party or third-party ones, are compatible and safe to use together. This is also the expectation consumers have when we purchase devices. If it has an USB-C port on it for charging, we expect that we can safely use any USB-C charger we have. In my opinion, non-compliant implementations of the USB-C standard should be prevented by regulation.

evidence_quotes.quote: I think it is unfair that consumers have to accept ads by default.
Please, let us prevent ads from tracking us.

paragraphs.text: I think it is unfair that consumers have to accept ads by default. Please, let us prevent ads from tracking us. Please, let us block ads if we so choose.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
21. Protect citizen's rights to refuse ads and block tracking.

paragraphs.text: A copyright infringement has been argued against ad blockers in a German court in order to make using ad blockers illegal. The argument is that ad blockers change the website’s code to block ads and that violates the site’s copyright. This is nonsense. However, the court might make a really unfortunate decision if it sides with the website’s owner. That would make ad blockers illegal.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
22. Guarantee legality of ad blockers.

evidence_quotes.quote: block sites from tracking us.

paragraphs.text: Ad blockers must remain legal to use, install, share, develop and distribute. Ad blockers let us to refuse intrusive and unwanted ads. They also help us to block sites from tracking us. Ad blockers are very important for security, privacy and convenience on the internet. Using ad blockers is not piracy or theft. Blocking ads is something consumers must be free to do.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
22. Guarantee legality of ad blockers.

paragraphs.text: Please, make sure ad blockers remain legal.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
22. Guarantee legality of ad blockers.

paragraphs.text: I read about a case on 18 August 2025, where an anime-art and adult-oriented content hosting platform "Gelbooru" was denied service by a company called "Cloud flare". Cloudflare provides cybersecurity services and protection against cyberattacks. Cloudflare denied service to Gelbooru, because they did not like the adult-only content Gelbooru is hosting. They thought doing business with Gelbooru would be a brand risk or a risk to their reputation. Withdrawal of service left Gelbooru vulnerable to cyberattacks.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
23. Prevent platforms and service providers from discriminating against businesses who involve themselves with only legal content.

evidence_quotes.quote: Cloudflare canceled their existing service to them.

paragraphs.text: As far as I know, the content on Gelbooru is legal. Yet, Cloudflare canceled their existing service to them. I think this is unfair. It falls to the law enforcement and the courts alone to determine what content is illegal, not to private companies or individuals. I also think service providers are abusing their power and they censor legal content they don't like, especially recently. They do this under the guise of brand and/or image protection.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
23. Prevent platforms and service providers from discriminating against businesses who involve themselves with only legal content.

evidence_quotes.quote: I think we need a tighter conditions for situations when a company can withdraw existing services from a business and what constitutes a reasonable or actionable brand risk.
service providers should be required to warn their customers about possible service withdrawal and provide genuine and accurate reasons for it.
Secondly, there should be a sufficient transition period, like 30 days, during which the service provider continues to provide the service after canceling it.
This way the effected business has time to find an alternative service provider.
These requirements would reduce service provider’s ability to abuse their power and status to censor businesses and legal content.

paragraphs.text: I think we need a tighter conditions for situations when a company can withdraw existing services from a business and what constitutes a reasonable or actionable brand risk. Based on these conditions, service providers should be required to warn their customers about possible service withdrawal and provide genuine and accurate reasons for it. Secondly, there should be a sufficient transition period, like 30 days, during which the service provider continues to provide the service after canceling it. This way the effected business has time to find an alternative service provider. These requirements would reduce service provider’s ability to abuse their power and status to censor businesses and legal content.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
23. Prevent platforms and service providers from discriminating against businesses who involve themselves with only legal content.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
23. Prevent platforms and service providers from discriminating against businesses who involve themselves with only legal content.

paragraphs.text: Please, protect legal content and legal art from censorship. Please, protect legal businesses from economic discrimination. Please, make sure that legal content hosted within the EU has the protection of the law. This is only fair to both consumers and businesses. And it will make the European market more appealing to companies to transition to and to operate in, when they know their legal content and legal businesses are protected.

section_path: 1. Require video game publishers to ensure that their games can be played even after official support ends.
23. Prevent platforms and service providers from discriminating against businesses who involve themselves with only legal content.

evidence_quotes.quote: By relying on companies outside the EU for storing and handling sensitive data, the EU institutions make themselves extremely vulnerable of being pressured by foreign actors, being spied on and being denied access to data and essential services.
Moreover, this reliance threatens citizens' rights and privacy as well, as weakened EU cannot protect them as well as it should.

paragraphs.text: By relying on companies outside the EU for storing and handling sensitive data, the EU institutions make themselves extremely vulnerable of being pressured by foreign actors, being spied on and being denied access to data and essential services. This vulnerability is a great threat to our democracy and sovereignty. I hope you agree with me on this. Moreover, this reliance threatens citizens' rights and privacy as well, as weakened EU cannot protect them as well as it should.

section_path: 24. Use only European companies, solutions and services for EU institutions' data storage, software solutions and cloud infrastructure.

evidence_quotes.quote: EU is using American cloud platforms, services and software instead equivalent offerings from European competitors.

paragraphs.text: I am both disappointed and worried, because, in my opinion, the EU institutions have not done enough to manage and lower this threat. EU is using American cloud platforms, services and software instead equivalent offerings from European competitors. We have cloud platforms, such as Nextcloud. We have email provider such as Proton. We have office applications such as Libre Office. We can use European solutions, if we want.

section_path: 24. Use only European companies, solutions and services for EU institutions' data storage, software solutions and cloud infrastructure.

paragraphs.text: When choosing non-European solutions, EU is, at best, wasting tax payer's money and, at worst, endangering our democracy and sovereignty. If EU would decide to switch to European solutions, it would increase self-reliance, protect the EU itself and its citizens. It would greatly promote European IT sector, create a lot of jobs and improve the quality of and variety of European software solutions.

section_path: 24. Use only European companies, solutions and services for EU institutions' data storage, software solutions and cloud infrastructure.

paragraphs.text: I suggest that, in the name of fairness and security, the EU institutions make a binding plan and a road-map to transit to European software solutions and platforms. After all, digital fairness for EU citizens does not depend on private and commercial actors alone, but on the EU as well.

section_path: 24. Use only European companies, solutions and services for EU institutions' data storage, software solutions and cloud infrastructure.

evidence_quotes.quote: They are vendor specific.
Only cartridges from the manufacturer of my printer can work.

paragraphs.text: Printer cartridges annoy me a lot. Especially annoying is how unfriendly they are for us consumers. They are vendor specific. I cannot use a cartridge from other vendors in my printer. Only cartridges from the manufacturer of my printer can work. And as if being vendor specific is not bad enough, most cartridges are also model specific. They will not fit in or work with all printers from the same manufacturer, but very specific models only.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers

evidence_quotes.quote: locking me in with my cartridge vendor.

paragraphs.text: This limits my choice as a consumer by locking me in with my cartridge vendor. I cannot just choose the cartridges I want, like I choose the paper, I am limited to specific vendor and specific

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers

paragraphs.text: Feedback for Digital Fairness Act 16 / 18

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers

evidence_quotes.quote: prizes higher than necessary.

paragraphs.text: model compatible cartridges. This also limits free and fair competition between companies and keeps prizes higher than necessary.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers

evidence_quotes.quote: compatible with all printer cartridges sold within the EU.

paragraphs.text: Please, require that all printers sold within the EU are compatible with all printer cartridges sold within the EU. This could be achieved with a standard cartridge interface implemented on cartridges and printers.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers

evidence_quotes.quote: my printer uses my colour cartridges as well as the black one.
This wastes colored ink.

paragraphs.text: Another thing with printer cartridges that annoys me is that when I print something in black-and-white only, my printer uses my colour cartridges as well as the black one. This wastes colored ink. Please, make sure printers stop doing that unless there is a very good reason for this behavior. And, if that is the case, then make manufacturers inform us about it in the manuals and packaging labels.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
26. Require black-and-white printing to only use black ink instead of colour cartridges

paragraphs.text: Modern printer cartridges have a chip on them which keeps track on ink and/or toner levels remaining in the cartridge and how old the cartridge is. The printer's firmware works together with these chips. This way, consumers should receive a notification if their printer is running low on ink or if the cartridge is very old and thus could contain bad ink.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
27. Ensure consumers can use all the ink and/or toner stored within their printer cartridges

evidence_quotes.quote: to disable printer cartridges, so that consumers have no choice but to buy new ones

paragraphs.text: However, this system is used maliciously by printer manufacturer to infringe on consumer rights. Instead of only informing users, this system is used to disable printer cartridges, so that consumers have no choice but to buy new ones even if the cartridges were still usable and had a lot of ink and/or toner left within them.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
27. Ensure consumers can use all the ink and/or toner stored within their printer cartridges

evidence_quotes.quote: the printer refuses to use it.
This creates unnecessary waste and harms consumers by planned obsolescence.
refilling a refillable cartridge might not reset the cartridge's age counter.

paragraphs.text: The cartridge might work just fine with what ink is left within, but the printer refuses to use it. It should be the consumer who chooses when to replace their cartridges and if the low ink levels affect the printing quality enough for them to do that. Same is with the old cartridges. They might work just fine even if they are old. It is the consumer who chooses when it is time to get new ones. Also, refilling a refillable cartridge might not reset the cartridge's age counter. This would soon disable the cartridge even though it is full and like new. This creates unnecessary waste and harms consumers by planned obsolescence.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
27. Ensure consumers can use all the ink and/or toner stored within their printer cartridges

evidence_quotes.quote: Please, require printer and cartridge manufacturers to remove this disabling functionality, so that consumers can use their printer cartridges to the last drop of ink.

paragraphs.text: Please, require printer and cartridge manufacturers to remove this disabling functionality, so that consumers can use their printer cartridges to the last drop of ink. Please, make sure consumers are only informed about ink and toner levels and about the cartridges' age. Please, also allow us to reset the age counter. Don't allow us to be pressures or forced to create unnecessary waste.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
27. Ensure consumers can use all the ink and/or toner stored within their printer cartridges

paragraphs.text: Feedback for Digital Fairness Act 17 / 18

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
28. Forbid tipping in the EU

evidence_quotes.quote: When I order food deliveries with a mobile app, I get asked if I would like to tip.

paragraphs.text: I live in Finland. We don't have the tipping culture here. Recently, I am both annoyed and worried because it seems to be spreading here as well. When I order food deliveries with a mobile app, I get asked if I would like to tip. I think this is wrong and unfair for several reasons.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
28. Forbid tipping in the EU

evidence_quotes.quote: tipping enables employers to cut costs unfairly and lower wages by transferring salary payment partly to customers.

paragraphs.text: Firstly, tipping is bad for workers. As has happened in America, tipping enables employers to cut costs unfairly and lower wages by transferring salary payment partly to customers. This puts workers in a vulnerable position. This also pressures other businesses to adopt tipping culture as a part of their business model, which worsens worker's position further. Tipping is common in low wage jobs too, which makes it even worse.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
28. Forbid tipping in the EU

evidence_quotes.quote: It does not feel good to get guilt-tripped into giving tips.

paragraphs.text: Secondly, tipping is unfair for the customers. It does not feel good to get guilt-tripped into giving tips. It is hard to refuse and refusing feels bad. This is emotional abuse. In my opinion, consumers should not be put into this position at all.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
28. Forbid tipping in the EU

paragraphs.text: In Finland, employers pay us living wages. We put out trust in them to do so and we have strong unions who negotiate salaries for us. We are proud of this system. It would be a great shame to see it degrade to the American level. Businesses and employers who cannot do business without this abusive practice, do not deserve to exist. We can do without them and will pay higher prizes if it is what it takes to stop the tipping culture.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
28. Forbid tipping in the EU

evidence_quotes.quote: tipping should be illegal in the EU.
tipping exploitation

paragraphs.text: For these reasons, I suggest that tipping should be illegal in the EU. At least in digital apps, but preferably in general. Please, protect our workers from tipping exploitation and businesses from unfair competition.

section_path: 25. Require printer cartridges to be inter changeable and compatible with all equivalent printers
28. Forbid tipping in the EU
